title: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence
wikitext:
[]: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 17, 2022 10:18 am Is it just me, or did they use the wrong hand in this schematic of New Unit 02? It says "palm" so the thumb is on the correct side, but it looks like the fingers are curling the wrong way. New 02 Angel Blood schematic  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by Blockio » Thu Mar 17, 2022 1:57 pm yup, that sure doesn't look natural. I suppose it could be at a really weird angle, but still. Ouch my wrists.

[]: Re: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 17, 2022 2:52 pm Hopefully it's something they catch and fix in the Blu-Ray release. Wonder if there's a way to let them know about this directly.

[]: Re: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by nerv bae » Fri Mar 18, 2022 11:25 am That's an interesting observation! But I don't think there is a mistake in the film. Instead, I think this is just an optical illusion like the spinning dancer : if you stare at the right hand for a bit, you can convince yourself either that the fingers are curled down (which would be a mistake) or curled up (which is not). I'm able to see it both ways.

[]: Re: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by Archer » Fri Mar 18, 2022 12:59 pm ^ yeah, more likely than not it’s just a perspective thing; the hand is actually facing palm-up. It looks like an unnatural position, but this doesn’t seem like an error that would’ve slipped through the cracks.

[]: Re: Mistake in Angel Blood sequence.             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Mar 18, 2022 1:14 pm nerv bae wrote: That's an interesting observation! But I don't think there is a mistake in the film. Instead, I think this is just an optical illusion like the spinning dancer : if you stare at the right hand for a bit, you can convince yourself either that the fingers are curled down (which would be a mistake) or curled up (which is not). I'm able to see it both ways.

 an interesting observation! But I don't think there is a mistake in the film. Instead, I think this is just an optical illusion like the spinning dancer : if you stare at the right hand for a bit, you can convince yourself either that the fingers are curled down (which would be a mistake) or curled up (which is not).
